mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP and retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >55% of periods where GDPC1 and RSAFS both show positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp federal funds rate hike triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, causing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following a 0.25% or larger funds rate increase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS increases by >100k and UNRATE falls by >0.2%, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production and housing starts reflect robust capacity utilization and construction activity, which sustains corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain flat or negative despite month-over-month increases in both INDPRO and HOUST exceeding 0.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden surge in retail sales indicates unexpected consumer strength that forces short sellers to cover positions, driving positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods where retail sales growth surpasses the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with stagnant payroll growth signals labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate demand and compresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive quarterly returns in >55% of observations when UNRATE increases by >0.3% and PAYEMS growth falls below 50k.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp expansions in industrial output during a recovery phase indicate rapid capacity utilization that boosts manufacturing earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >60% of months where industrial production jumps above 0.8% following a recessionary trough.
